text: In this video, we would like to present lift procedure for transphacteric posterior anal fistula. A sixty-four years old gentleman presented at our clinic complaining of constant secretion through opening posterior from the anus. According to Park's classification, fistula in our case is uncomplicated transsphincteric fistula.
The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position with the buttocks taped aside. A water test is conducted to visually confirm the presence of an internal opening of the anal fistula.
The surgeon inserts a proctoscope into the anal canal and repeats the water test to confirm the presence of an internal opening.
The surgeon probes the fistula tract and makes an incision above the intersphincteric groove, identifying the intersphincteric space.
The surgeon identifies and dissects the fistula tract, then ligates it using 3-0 micro-interrupted sutures, securing the tract to prevent further secretion.
The surgeon transects the fistula tract and curettes it, followed by ligation of the other end using SAMe-III-O Vicryl interrupted sutures.
The surgeon tightly ligates the fistula tract and electrocauterizes the internal opening of the fistula. The skin defect is then closed using three interrupted sutures.
